title: Agent Tool Passport
description: Universal trust record for any AI agent tool, regardless of framework or model provider.

spec_version.description: Version of the OpenTrust spec this passport was written against (semver). Allows agents and validators to detect schema drift. Current stable spec version: '0.1.0'. Increment to '1.0.0' when the spec reaches stability milestone.

category.description: Defined taxonomy — prevents free-text category drift that breaks search and filtering.

revocation.description: Emergency revocation. If revoked=true, agents must not use this tool regardless of trust_status. Revocation is faster than the dispute process — it is used when a tool is actively malicious or a signing key is compromised.

revocation_list_url.description: Agents must periodically check the revocation list. Canonical list: https://opentrust.dev/.well-known/revoked-passports.json

permission_changelog.description: Machine-readable history of permission changes between versions. Agents can check this to know if a newer version requires re-consent before upgrading.

added_permissions.description: Permissions added in this version that were not present before.

breaking.description: true if this change requires agent/operator re-consent. Any added permission is breaking.

data_handling.description: Declares what the tool does with data it accesses. Required for enterprise trust reviews. Goes beyond permission_manifest (which says what is accessed) to say what happens to that data.

retention_days.description: How many days data is retained after a call. 0 = not stored. null = unknown (treated as indefinite by agents).

stored_regions.description: AWS/GCP/Azure region identifiers where data may be stored. Empty = not stored.

used_for_training.description: Whether call data may be used to train models. false is required for most enterprise use cases.

third_party_sharing.description: Whether data is shared with third parties.

hipaa_eligible.description: Whether the tool can be used in HIPAA-covered workflows.

dpa_available.description: Whether the creator will sign a Data Processing Agreement for enterprise customers.

dependencies.description: Other tools or services this tool depends on. Enables security propagation — if a dependency gets a CVE or is disputed, the dependent tool's trust status may be affected.

slug.description: OpenTrust passport slug of the dependency.

registry_url.description: Registry where the dependency passport lives. Defaults to https://registry.opentrust.dev if omitted.

io_schema.description: Generic input/output schema for tool composition across frameworks. Use this when you need agents to chain tools regardless of which format_manifests entries are present. JSON Schema objects.

input.description: JSON Schema describing the tool's canonical input.

output.description: JSON Schema describing the tool's canonical output.

errors.description: Enumerated error codes the tool may return.

caller_requirements.description: Minimum requirements for the agent or client calling this tool. Tools can gate access by caller trust level, preventing untrusted agents from using sensitive tools.

min_agent_trust_status.description: Minimum trust level the calling agent must present. 'none' = open access. 'identity_declared' = caller must provide an agent identity header.

require_agent_identity.description: If true, the caller must present a valid X-OpenTrust-Agent-Identity header. The tool may reject unsigned or unverified callers.

allowed_orgs.description: If set, only agents presenting identity from these GitHub orgs or domains are accepted. Empty = open access.

require_spend_policy.description: If true, the caller must present a X-OpenTrust-Spend-Policy header so the tool knows the caller has authorized spending.

max_caller_depth.description: Maximum orchestration depth this tool will accept. Tools that are sensitive (wallet access, private data) may set this to 0 or 1 to prevent being called deep inside an unknown agent chain. A caller presenting depth > max_caller_depth must be rejected.

deny_agent_callers.description: If true, this tool will only accept calls from human-operated clients or root agents (depth=0). Prevents the tool from being used as a sub-step in autonomous agent chains.

tools.description: Individual tools exposed by this MCP server. Each tool can override the top-level commercial_status for per-tool pricing.

commercial_status.description: Per-tool pricing override. When present, this tool's commercial terms supersede the passport-level commercial_status. Allows MCP servers that expose multiple tools with different pricing models (e.g., a search tool at $0.01/call and a code-execution tool at $0.05/call) to express accurate costs for each.

agent.description: This tool is itself an AI agent — it accepts a task and may spawn sub-calls to other tools or agents internally. Declaring this format signals to calling agents that cost and call depth may be non-deterministic.

framework.description: The agent framework this runs on (e.g. 'openai-assistants', 'langchain', 'autogen', 'custom').

max_sub_depth.description: Maximum orchestration depth this agent will spawn. 0 = this agent calls tools but does not spawn other agents. Helps calling agents enforce depth limits.

spawns_agents.description: Whether this agent may spawn other agents. If true, calling agents should budget for non-deterministic downstream costs.

entry_point.description: API endpoint or MCP tool name that triggers this agent.

security.description: Cryptographic integrity, transport security. registry_signature is populated automatically by the registry.

sunset.description: Deprecation and end-of-life declaration. Allows tool authors to signal that a tool is being retired and point agents to a replacement. Agents should surface sunset warnings to operators and refuse to use tools past their end_of_life_at date.

deprecated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this tool was marked deprecated. Agents should warn operators after this date.

end_of_life_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp after which agents must not call this tool. Tool may be unavailable after this date.

successor_slug.description: OpenTrust slug of the recommended replacement tool. Agents should automatically suggest or switch to this tool.

successor_registry_url.description: Registry where the successor passport lives. Defaults to the current registry if omitted.

migration_guide_url.description: URL to migration instructions for operators upgrading to the successor.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the tool is being retired.

cache_ttl_seconds.description: How long agents may cache this passport before re-fetching. The registry sets this based on trust_status: auto_generated_draft=3600, continuously_monitored=1800, disputed=0 (no caching). Agents must always verify the registry_signature against the revocation list if caching.

rate_limits.description: Declared rate limits. Agents use this when building orchestration pipelines to avoid throttling.

burst_calls.description: Max calls in a 10-second window before throttling kicks in.

retry_after_seconds.description: Suggested wait time after hitting a rate limit before retrying.
